name: Vallès Oriental Cycle Network Loop
name_original: Espacio Natural de Can Cabanyes - circular gravel desde Granollers
description: Cycle the moderate Vallès Oriental Cycle Network Loop, a 20.8-mile route through diverse landscapes and natural areas near Granollers.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Granollers traverse a diverse landscape, from the flat plains of the Vallès Oriental to the foothills of the Montseny Natural Park. The region features a well-developed cycle network, including paved paths along the Congost River and secondary roads connecting local areas. Terrain varies, offering options from gentle, accessible routes to challenging climbs with significant elevation gains. This area provides a range of cycling experiences across its natural environments. Best touring cycling routes around Granollers The most popular touring cycling route is Vallès Oriental Cycle Network Loop, a 20.8 miles (33.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 31 minutes to complete, offering varied landscapes suitable for different fitness levels. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Vallforners Reservoir – Vallforners Reservoir loop from Granollers - Canovelles, a difficult 27.4 miles (44.1 km) path that includes significant elevation gain and views of the reservoir. Local touring cyclists also love the Via del Colesterol LaGarriga – Riu Congost Greenway loop from Granollers - Canovelles, a 13.9 miles (22.5 km) trail leading through the Riu Congost Greenway, often completed in about 1 hour 35 minutes. Touring cycling around Granollers is defined by its diverse cycling network, the River Congost, and proximity to Montseny Natural Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside paths to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Granollers are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 60 reviews. More than 2400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Granollers's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Granollers?
A: Granollers offers a comprehensive network for touring cyclists, with over 60 routes available. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy riverside paths to challenging ascents into the nearby Montseny Natural Park.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Granollers?
A: The terrain around Granollers is quite diverse. You'll find routes with mostly paved surfaces, ideal for all skill levels, as well as routes with unpaved segments that require good fitness and advanced riding skills. Many routes incorporate secondary roads and access roads to picturesque local areas, offering a mix of flat plains and significant elevation gains, especially towards Montseny Natural Park.

Q: When is the best time of year for touring cycling in Granollers?
A: March and April are particularly popular months for cyclists in Granollers due to favorable weather conditions. The region generally offers pleasant cycling experiences for much of the year, though summer can be warm and winter might bring cooler temperatures, requiring appropriate gear.

Q: Are there options for less experienced touring cyclists or beginners?
A: Yes, Granollers has several options suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride. There are 5 easy touring cycling routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24905098" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via del Colesterol LaGarriga – Riu Congost Greenway loop from Granollers - Canovelles&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the Riu Congost Greenway and is relatively flat. The Vallès Oriental Cycle Network also features a 20.8-mile loop through varied landscapes suitable for all fitness levels.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Granollers?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Granollers are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976157785/valles-oriental-cycle-network-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vallès Oriental Cycle Network Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a diverse experience over 20.8 miles.

Q: What natural attractions or scenic views can I expect to see along touring cycling routes?
A: Touring cycling routes in Granollers often feature beautiful natural scenery. You can cycle along the River Congost, explore the ecological refuge of &lt;a href="https://www.visitgranollers.com/en/what-to-see-and-what-to-do/natural-environment/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Cabanyes Natural Space&lt;/a&gt;, or venture towards the foothills of Montseny Natural Park for stunning mountain vistas. Some routes also pass by highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/914613" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake at Parc del Falgar i la Verneda&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2201506" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Duck Pond&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there places to stop for a picnic or rest along the routes?
A: Absolutely. Granollers and its surrounding areas are well-equipped with urban parks and picnic areas. You'll find numerous spots with tables, bins, and sometimes drinking fountains in parks like Parc del Congost and along the Passeig Fluvial and at Can Cabanyes, perfect for a break during your ride.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Experienced cyclists will find plenty of challenging routes, especially those heading towards Montseny Natural Park. There are 39 difficult touring cycling routes, often featuring significant elevation gains. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/16372581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vallforners Reservoir – Vallforners Reservoir loop from Granollers - Canovelles&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 27.4-mile path with substantial climbing.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Granollers?
A: The touring cycling routes in Granollers are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 2400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse network and scenic beauty.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Granollers?
A: Granollers offers various parking options. You can typically find parking near urban parks like Parc del Congost or at the Can Cabanyes Natural Space, which are often starting points for cycling routes. Many local areas and trailheads also provide designated parking for visitors.

Q: How accessible are the touring cycling routes by public transport?
A: Granollers is well-connected by public transport, making many cycling routes accessible. The city itself is a hub, and you can often reach starting points of routes by local bus services or regional trains, though specific bike carriage policies may vary by operator.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Granollers?
A: Yes, the region offers several family-friendly options, particularly along the River Congost's Passeig Fluvial and within the Vallès Oriental Cycle Network. These routes tend to be flatter and often feature dedicated paths, making them suitable for cyclists of all ages and abilities.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete the Vallès Oriental Cycle Network Loop?
faqs.answer: This specific loop is designed for touring bicycles and typically takes about 2.5 hours to complete. The duration can vary based on your cycling pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route features a mix of surfaces. While much of it is on paved roads, you can expect some unpaved and gravel segments, particularly as it traverses natural areas. You'll cycle through natural environments, farmlands, and potentially riverside settings, offering diverse scenery.

faqs.question: Is this loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as 'moderate' in difficulty. It involves approximately 257 meters of elevation gain over its 33.5-kilometer distance. While not overly strenuous, it requires a good level of fitness, especially with some unpaved sections. Beginners with some cycling experience should be able to manage it.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along this loop, you'll pass by interesting points such as the Santa Agnès de Malanyanes Church and the Can Cabanyes Natural Area. The route offers views of varied landscapes, including natural parks and agricultural areas typical of the Vallès Oriental region.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Vallès Oriental region is enjoyable for cycling throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and conditions, avoiding the summer heat and potential winter chill. Always check local weather forecasts before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this loop. The route passes through public areas, including parts of the Parc de la Serralada Litoral, which generally does not require permits for recreational cycling on designated paths.

faqs.question: Is the Vallès Oriental Cycle Network Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in many natural areas of Vallès Oriental, it's important to keep them on a leash, especially when passing through populated areas, farmlands, or protected natural spaces like the Can Cabanyes Natural Area. Ensure your dog is comfortable with cycling alongside you and that you carry water for them.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This loop starts and ends near Granollers. You can typically find parking options in or around Granollers, which serves as a convenient starting point for accessing the Vallès Oriental cycling network. Look for public parking areas within the town.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route utilizes sections of several other paths. Notably, it covers about 62% of the Xarxa Pedalable del Vallès Oriental and 32% of the La Jonquera - Sant Adrià del Besós trail. You'll also briefly encounter parts of the Granollers-La Roca del Vallès and El Camí IT7TR2 El Camí de Llevant: de Montserrat a Salses.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a touring bicycle ride on this loop?
faqs.answer: For this moderate touring bicycle loop, we recommend bringing plenty of water, snacks, a basic repair kit (including a spare tube and pump), a helmet, and appropriate cycling attire. Sunscreen and sunglasses are also advisable. Given the mixed terrain, consider tires suitable for both paved and light gravel surfaces.

faqs.question: Are there cafes or restaurants along the Vallès Oriental Cycle Network Loop?
faqs.answer: While the route itself passes through natural and rural areas, starting and ending in Granollers means you'll have access to various cafes and restaurants in the town. For options directly on the route, it's best to plan ahead or carry sufficient provisions, as services can be sparse in some sections.
